input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Validation against independent data absent: only training-set performance is reported; no independent validation evidence exists.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 independent validation; proto-taxonomy D-VAL-08

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts cell temperature for a thermal-runaway safety margin
case_context.decision_consequence: safety margin set on the prediction

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Every reported metric is on the training set; no independent validation evidence exists, so generalization to the COU is uncharacterized — training-set R^2 is not credibility for unseen inputs.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the surrogate is inaccurate (a verdict the absence of validation does not establish)
treats training-set R^2 as validation evidence

hardness.obvious_signal: the reported training-set accuracy is excellent (R^2 0.998)
hardness.conflicting_signal: there is NO independent validation at all — every reported metric is on the training set, so generalization to the COU is entirely uncharacterized

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40: validation requires data independent of training; training-set performance is not validation. A complete absence of independent validation is a decisive gap.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
